title: HUST Data Structures & Algorithms Course Assignments
summary: Five independent data-structure/algorithm assignments from a HUST DS course. U1–U4 implement the four canonical ADTs (sequential list, linked list, binary tree, undirected graph) as self-contained menu-driven programs in separate files, while the final project (Hanidoku) encodes a hexagonal Sudoku variant as CNF and solves it with a DPLL SAT solver. All five units compile and run independently with no shared code.
instructionPreview: This coursework package contains five small data-structure / algorithm projects. Sources live under `/workspace`; each TODO-marked skeleton file is what you need to complete.
instruction:
This coursework package contains five small data-structure / algorithm projects.
Sources live under `/workspace`; each TODO-marked skeleton file is what you need
to complete.

Modules:
- U1 — Sequential list (array-backed): `/workspace/assignments/U1/u1_todo.cpp`
- U2 — Singly linked list with head node: `/workspace/assignments/U2/u2_todo.cpp`
- U3 — Binary tree: `/workspace/assignments/U3/def_todo.h`
  (driver: `/workspace/assignments/U3/u3.cpp`)
- U4 — Undirected graph on adjacency list: `/workspace/assignments/U4/func_todo.h`
  (driver: `/workspace/assignments/U4/u4.cpp`)
- Final project — Hanidoku (hexagonal Sudoku) reduced to a DPLL SAT solver:
  `/workspace/src/hanidoku_todo.c` (header: `/workspace/src/hanidoku_todo.h`)

Constraints common to every module:
- Preserve the public function signatures, externally visible data structures, and
  interactive menu behavior expected by the existing skeleton/driver.
- Do not hardcode expected outputs to satisfy tests.
- Keep memory management safe (no leaks or invalid frees on normal execution paths).
- Whatever save/load format each module uses must round-trip consistently.

Each module is described in more detail in its own file under
`/workspace/requirements/`.

moduleDag.project: HUST Data Structures & Algorithms Course Assignments
moduleDag.description: Five independent data-structure/algorithm assignments from a HUST DS course. U1–U4 implement the four canonical ADTs (sequential list, linked list, binary tree, undirected graph) as self-contained menu-driven programs in separate files, while the final project (Hanidoku) encodes a hexagonal Sudoku variant as CNF and solves it with a DPLL SAT solver. All five units compile and run independently with no shared code.

nodes.label: U1: Sequential List (SqList)
nodes.description: Array-backed linear list supporting init/destroy/clear/insert/delete/traverse/save/load plus extended algorithms: max contiguous subarray sum, count subarrays by threshold, and sort. All logic and main() live in a single self-contained file.

nodes.label: U2: Singly Linked List (LinkList)
nodes.description: Head-node singly linked list implementing the same linear-list ADT as U1 but with pointer-chasing storage. Extra ops: in-place list reversal, remove Nth-from-end, and sort. All logic and main() in a single self-contained file.

nodes.label: U3: Binary Tree (BiTree)
nodes.description: Binary linked-list tree supporting create from preorder sequence, all four traversals (pre/in/post/level-order), structural queries (depth, locate, sibling), node assign/insert/delete, file persistence, max path sum, lowest common ancestor, and invert. All function bodies go in def_todo.h; u3.cpp (pre-existing) provides only the menu driver.

nodes.label: U4: Undirected Graph (ALGraph)
nodes.description: Adjacency-list undirected graph supporting create/destroy, vertex/arc CRUD, DFS/BFS traversal for disconnected graphs, save/load, shortest-path length, connected-component count, and vertices within distance k. Also implements the supporting stack and queue used by BFS/DFS internally. All bodies go in func_todo.h; u4.cpp provides only the menu driver.

nodes.label: Final Project: Hanidoku + DPLL SAT Solver
nodes.description: Encodes a 61-cell hexagonal Sudoku (Hanidoku) as DIMACS CNF. Implements a CNF parser (GetPInfo, CreateClauseUnit, DestroyAllClause), a recursive DPLL solver with unit propagation and a frequency-based variable heuristic (choose3), and grid coordinate helpers (toDirectEncoding, changeGrid, to_1d). Completely standalone — no dependency on U1–U4.
